title: Pneumonanthe remota
taxonomy: Pneumonanthe remota is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It belongs to the genus Pneumonanthe, which is a group of plants that are commonly known as fog gentians. The plant is native to the Himalayas and is characterized by its unique ability to thrive in foggy environments. Pneumonanthe remota is a rare and endangered species due to habitat destruction and other environmental factors. As a result, it is considered a species of special concern by conservation organizations.

The medicinal properties of Pneumonanthe remota are not well-documented, but some research suggests its potential in treating various health conditions.
